channel.title: Comments for page "Acquisitions - a Marshall, Carter & Dark Tale"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Acquisitions - a Marshall, Carter & Dark Tale"

item.title: Well Damn
item.content:encoded: This series is actually one of the most well-written, well thought out and just all around best tales I have ever read, much less on this site. This could legitimately be a published short story and would not be out of place in a collection of sci-fi short stories (maybe with a few of the obscure scp references changed or removed). While this may be somewhat of a recropost, I feel like this is a shining example of what writing for this site can be like, and congratulations SRegan, you have created a fantastic work of art.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Almost five years later, I still consider this one of the SCP Wiki's masterpieces. I know it's long, but you guys owe it to yourselves to read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like The Firm spliced together with some of the most subtle dark fantasy. I love this.
Great job to one of the greatest tale series on the site. Pls come back SRegan ;_;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would love to read more about Edward Gradley, he's a deeply sympathetic hero, and I kind of love him to pieces.

BTW, I'm not intimidated by long stories (big Stephen King fan here, heh), and this one took me about an hour and a half.

I'm off to your author page now, have all 4 of my upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Read through the entire thing, excellent, upvoted. Read the prelude, just realized what the Russian wanted… clicked the upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely fantastic. It's been on my "to read" list for a while now, and I admit I was hesitant to dive in due to it's length. Now, I'm kicking myself for not having read it sooner. Once I started, I just fell into it, and couldn't stop myself from reading the whole thing. It is easily one of my favorite things on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Finally read this through, and it's really as amazing as everyone says… which is neat, because usually when this happens [everyone says something really long is awesome] I'm left disappointed with the feeling that people upvoted due to being impressed by the length… XD

But this is really just that good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just so good. Just great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this entire thing during a road trip to California earlier this year. It was a blast to read and a great way to pass the time. Hat's off to you, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a chronic lurker, I almost resent the author of this piece for forcing me not only to go through the application process, but to also lavish praise, solely through writing talent. Good job, and I hope to see more soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd buy this in a book store. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty sure I've said this before, in several places, and in chat, but this is one of the finest examples of fiction on this site.
THE BEST
This definitely deserved to get featured, and it is a tale that I plan to wave in many faces for a long, long time.
And maybe Unfinished Business, if it wasn't so unfinished
This is excellent, SRegan, and you should feel good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congrats to SRegan indeed.
As far as I'm concerned, one of the best reads we have on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And it's about time this got featured. I've enjoyed this ever since it was first posted. Congrats, SRegan!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this Tale. The way that it brings together so many disparate elements scattered throughout the site, to form something something so whole and awesome, is … well, it's the reason I joined the site. This Tale specifically.

(I hope that evokes feelings of pride, rather than guilt.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All of my internets are ceded to you, sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't this been upvoted through the roof yet? This was just… amazing. Probably the best thing I've read on the site.
Very, very well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would buy this as a bound paperback. It's that good. This is my favorite thing I've read on the site thus far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. Seeing more longer fiction on the site makes me feel happy inside. This was seriously incredible. All of my +1s are to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic stuff, between this and the recent 'Conspiracy' by Hornby we've definitely had some cracking reads lately. It'll be hard to top you, in length at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is probably one of the best tales I have ever read on this site, at the very least I think it would take gold for the longest. It created an in-depth atmosphere, gave a close look at an intriguing GOI, as well as having a genuine air of darkness about it. Not only did you make the characters have depth, you also managed to make the dialog seem natural (even with the action-movie-like part at the end), something a lot of articles and tales do not pull off as masterfully as this did. When I finally start writing stuff, I will be happy having written something half as good as this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He just did…it's called Acquisitions - a Marshall, Carter & Dark Tale

:P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
BEST TALE EVAR!!!

Good god man, sit down and write a book please. You have too much talent not to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ….you did something this long and this good in your free time over the course of about a month and a half? That's DISGUSTING, who do you think you are anyway?! :P sigh….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finished reading it and was not disappointed. You may just have written the definitive MC&D tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers! I drafted the outline on and off over about a month - the actual writing took me a long weekend (bank holiday ftw), the week's evenings, then most of the next weekend :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. I'm not even a quarter of the way through it and it reads like a cross between Angela Carter and Methods of Rationality. And the epigraph…hnngh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I started reading the first one while I was snagging a quick bite, and ended up sitting in the parking lot reading Act I until it was done.

So yeah, good job on these. Enjoyed the whole read all the way through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it gets better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay. At the risk of giving excessively early praise, l love the fuck out of this.
I upvoted the first episode after reading the letter ,then wanted to upvote again when the jellybean episode happened, and then two-three times again.
So far, this is brilliant, and the way you write, especially the abovementioned episode, reminded me a lot of Roald Dahl's short stories.
Well done, very well done - I take off my flatcap at you in respect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i havent even read them yet, all i've done so far is open them. as soon as i saw how long they were, and the sort of style you were writing in, i upvoted every page :P now to hope that that is not rescinded by the contents. don't disappoint me

how long did this take you?

*edit*

disappointment: non-existent. this was absolutely amazing. looks to be 44,000 - 45,000 words all told….so about 127 pages for your average paperback. double that and you've got a bonafide novel on your hands. you have an in-depth, coherent, and even devious plot, plenty of intrigue, interesting characters, and dramatic tension that kept me wanting more. you properly used a wide variety of SCP materials, to the point that i found myself going back to review the entries in question to see just how well you HAD used them.

the only thing i worry about is that it's so long that you won't get many who finish the entire thing. if that's the case, don't get discouraged! you are a pretty awesome writer here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoting with much glee. I love these.
